events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Let's parse image. Activities with durations and dependencies. Disruption: Activity N (Paint) needs 3 extra workdays. Need project finish delay in workdays, whether completion slips, what drives answer.

We need compute original project duration and new duration after N duration increases from 4d to 7d. Need check if float absorbs. Since N is on critical path likely. Let's compute.

Schedule early-start bars. Let's list activities:
A Mobilize & layout [2d] start 0-2
B Underground rough-in [3d] pred A, start 2-5
C Slab pour [1d] pred B, start 5-6
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C, start 6-9 (hatched)
D Framing [4d] pred CC, start 9-13
E Roof dry-in [2d] pred D, start 13-15
F Electrical rough [6d] pred E, start 15-21
G Plumbing top-out [4d] pred E, start 15-19
H HVAC rough [4d] pred E, start 15-19
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H, start 21-22 (red)
J Insulation [2d] pred I, start 22-24
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J, start 24-25 (red)
L Drywall hang [3d] pred K, start 25-28
M Tape & finish [3d] pred L, start 28-31
N Paint [4d] pred M, start 31-35
O Trim carpentry [4d] pred N, start 35-39
P Flooring [2d] pred N, start 35-37
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P, start 39-40 (red)

Original finish workday 40.

N becomes 7d: start 31, finish 38. O starts 38 finish 42. P starts 38 finish 40. Q starts 42 finish 43. Delay = 3 workdays. No float because path M-N-O-Q critical. Also P has float? P finish 40 originally, Q starts 39 due O. Actually Q pred O,P; O drives. P has 2 days float? P 35-37, Q 39-40, P float 2. But O drives. N delay pushes O, so delay 3.

Need identify what drives answer: N is on critical path; successor O and final Q. Completion slips by 3 workdays.

Need output JSON with required fields. decision? This is schedule impact assessment, not pass/fail inspection. The task says decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid. But required output shape includes decision pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since disruption causes delay but not dangerous/non-compliant. Could be "needs_more_info"? We are to report delay. Maybe decision "fail" if schedule non-compliant? But no. The instruction: If work dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Here not. Could use "pass"? But schedule delay is not a pass/fail component condition. Maybe "needs_more_info" because recovery plan not fully specified? However task asks report project finish delay. I think decision can be "needs_more_info" or "pass". Let's think.

The scenario: commercial construction, schedule & constraint reasoning. We assess real trade work. We need decide what work can proceed, what is blocked, and what recovery plan is valid. The disruption: Paint needs 3 extra days. At stage in-progress. We can conclude completion slips 3 days. Workable activities? Those not dependent on N and not already completed? The task asks use workable for list of activity IDs when task asks what work can still start. It does ask "decide what work can proceed, what is blocked". So include workable list. At current stage in-progress, activities before N may be in progress/completed. But from schedule, maybe M is in progress? Stage under review is in-progress. We don't know exact current date. But we can infer from lookahead schedule. The question: "Read the schedule and the disruption from the image. Report the project finish delay in workdays in the value field (0 if the float absorbs it), state whether the completion date slips, and identify what drives your answer." It doesn't explicitly ask for workable list, but the context says decide what work can proceed. The output schema includes workable. We should populate.

Need determine which activities can proceed. Since N delayed, any activity not predecessor-dependent on N and with predecessors complete can proceed. But we don't know current date. The schedule is lookahead. If we are at disruption point (N needs extra days), M likely just completed or in progress? Actu…
